Add to this Chinese domestic product inventory declines.

One way or another, there is a lot less oil/products in China today, than in February.

Remember these imports are customs records (the actual agreement to purchase was probably ~2 months earlier).#OOTT
quoted_text: Since the US/Iran war began, Chinese imports have plummeted, falling by 3.5MM Bbl/d YOY on avg. April-June and down 4.9MM Bbl/d YOY in June alone.  This is not "demand destruction" but rather intentional rebalancing as the net reduction of 320MM Bbls from April to June (!!!) offset ~26% of the forfeited 1.2BN barrels of Middle Eastern production over the same period. Despite this historic import drop and the largest SPR release in history, global onshore oil inventories STILL fell by a whopping

text: #Venezuela's 645,000-barrel-per-day Amuay refinery, the country's largest, was back in service on Friday after a power outage. The refinery is processing some 140,000 bpd of crude and its fluid catalytic cracker is active, three sources from the facility said. #oott https://t.co/mFb915t7IM
